# doctor-all: af doctor --all re-executes the real binary against an
# isolated scratch HOME. The global PATH fakes (git/tmux/pi/...) stand
# in for host tools, so the whole self-smoke runs hermetically; the
# scratch root lives under the system temp dir and is removed by the
# command itself.
#
# Scenarios:
#   1. af doctor --all --report writes md+json reports and exits 0.
#   2. The summary line reports zero failures.
#   3. Failure: --report-dir pointing at an unwritable path errors.

env HOME=$WORK/home
mkdir $WORK/reports

# Smart fake git: answers the queries af create/done issue during the
# smoke (toplevel = cwd, no remotes, everything else succeeds quietly),
# so the whole lifecycle runs hermetically.
mkdir $WORK/smartgit
cp smartgit.sh $WORK/smartgit/git
exec chmod +x $WORK/smartgit/git
env PATH=$WORK/smartgit:$PATH

exec af doctor --all --report --report-dir $WORK/reports
stdout 'doctor self-smoke: \d+ pass, 0 fail, \d+ skip'
stdout 'report: '
exists $WORK/reports

# exactly one md and one json artifact
exec sh -c 'ls $WORK/reports/af-doctor-smoke-*.md | wc -l'
stdout '^\s*1$'
exec sh -c 'ls $WORK/reports/af-doctor-smoke-*.json | wc -l'
stdout '^\s*1$'
exec sh -c 'grep -c "## Passed" $WORK/reports/af-doctor-smoke-*.md'
stdout '^\s*1$'

# Failure: report dir blocked by an existing file
exec sh -c 'touch $WORK/blocked'
! exec af doctor --all --report --report-dir $WORK/blocked/sub
stderr 'report'

-- smartgit.sh --
#!/bin/sh
case "$1 $2" in
"rev-parse --show-toplevel") pwd ;;
"config --get") exit 1 ;;
*) exit 0 ;;
esac
